title: allionia incarnata
intro: Allionia incarnata, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce vomiting and relieve symptoms of fever, due to the presence of toxic compounds like diterpenoid alkaloids. Its health benefits include treating conditions such as coughs, colds, and even certain types of cancer, although further research is needed to confirm the latter. Herbal preparations of Allionia incarnata often involve drying and processing the roots and leaves to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
